what_it_does: Implements the `company board` CLI subcommand: an inward-facing noticeboard interface that lists, files, reads, and transitions typed board items stored as git-tracked markdown, delegating all storage logic to `runtime/cc_board.py`.
observations: Manual argv parsing with `iter(args)` and `next()` instead of `argparse`
Imports `runtime.cc_board` by mutating `sys.path` relative to the file location
`file` builds links from `--link kind:target` specs and passes them to `cb.file_item`
Output is plain-text formatted rows and full item detail including links and history
Top docstring explains the cross-session request-loop concept
standouts: Treats separate CLI sessions as the cross-session request loop for the board
Uses a `board://<id>` addressing scheme for file-based items
Pure file I/O facade: no service, just CLI over `runtime/cc_board.py`
conventions: Follows a simple subcommand dispatch pattern in `run(args)`
Uses module-level `REPO` path injection for sibling-package imports
concerns: Manual argument parsing is brittle: missing flag values become `None` without clear errors
No `--help` or usage printing beyond error exits
`links` and `history` assume specific dict shapes without local validation
notes: This is the thin CLI wrapper around the Company noticeboard runtime, handling operator-facing list/file/get/transition commands.
questions: What is the exact API contract and exception shape of `runtime/cc_board.py`?
How are board item IDs generated and what lifecycle states are valid?
What is the on-disk markdown schema in `channel-memory/noticeboard/<id>.md`?
How does `cb.list_items` implement the `--author` filter against `author_session`?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it exposes a CLI for listing, filing, reading, and transitioning board items.
intention_signals: Provide a low-friction, inward-facing command-line surface for filing and moving noticeboard items, treating the CLI itself as the cross-session request mechanism.
novelty: The explicit framing that `company board file` in one session and `company board list/transition` in another constitutes the request loop, rather than a long-running service.
contribution: A CLI dispatcher and formatter for board item operations, translating command-line flags into calls to the board runtime and rendering items, lists, and transition results for operators.
summary_for_embedding: `company/ops/cli/board.py` is the CLI frontend for the Company noticeboard, dispatching `list`, `file`, `get`, `transition`, and `types` subcommands to `runtime/cc_board.py` and formatting plain-text output.
intention_for_embedding: To give operators a command-line way to file, inspect, filter, and advance noticeboard items stored as git-tracked markdown.

symbols.does: Formats a board item as a single-line summary row for list output.
symbols.description: Takes an item dict and returns a left-aligned string containing the id, type, state, author session, truncated title, and a link count indicator.

symbols.does: Prints the full detail of a board item to stdout.
symbols.description: Renders id, address, type/state/source, title, author/channel/thread, timestamps, links, history, and body in a structured plain-text format.

symbols.does: Dispatches `company board` subcommands and parses their flags.
symbols.description: Implements the main entrypoint for `list`, `types`, `file`, `get`, and `transition` subcommands, manually parsing argv, calling `runtime/cc_board.py` functions, and printing results or errors.
